```tactics
import FLT.AutomorphicForm.QuaternionAlgebra.HeckeOperators.Concrete
import FLT.DedekindDomain.IntegralClosure
import FLT.Deformations.RepresentationTheory.GaloisRep
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.Cyclotomic.CyclotomicCharacter
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.Padics.Complex
import Mathlib.RingTheory.SimpleRing.Principal

/-!
# Automorphic Galois representations

For our proof of Fermat's Last Theorem, the following definition is the most convenient.

We say that a 2-dimensional $p$-adic or a mod $p$ Galois representation of the absolute
Galois of a totally real field number field $F$ of even degree is *automorphic* if there
exists a totally definite quaternion algebra $D/F$ unramified at all finite places,
a finite set $S$ of finite places of $F$, and an automorphic form of level $U_1(S)$
(matrices congruent to $(a *;0 a)$ mod $v$ for all $v\in S$) and weight 2 for $D$
such that the Galois representation is associated to the form by the construction of
Carayol, Taylor et al.

## Notes

Several things here are specialized to our case. We demand that the quaternion algebra is
unramified everywhere because this is the only case that we need. We stick to weight 2
because this is the only case that we need. The level is also more restrictive but again
the only thing we need: the automorphic forms it catches are trivial at all infinite places
and at all finite places are either Steinberg or principal series `π(χ₁, χ₂)` with `χᵢ` tame
and `χ₁χ₂` unramified.

-/


open scoped TensorProduct

open IsDedekindDomain NumberField TotallyDefiniteQuaternionAlgebra.WeightTwoAutomorphicForm

local notation "Frob" => Field.AbsoluteGaloisGroup.adicArithFrob
local notation3 "Γ" K:max => Field.absoluteGaloisGroup K
local notation3 K:max "ᵃˡᶠ" => AlgebraicClosure K

universe u -- u for number field / quaternion algebra.

set_option linter.unusedVariables false in -- we don't assume p is prime, p in A,
-- dim(V) = 2 etc etc in the definition itself, but it would be mathematically ridiculous
-- to leave these assumptions out of the definition.

/--
A 2-dimensional Galois representation `ρ` of the absolute Galois of a totally
real field of even degree is said to be automorphic if there's a quaternion algebra D over F of discriminant 1
and an `A`-valued automorphic eigenform, by which we mean a ℤ_p-linear map from the ℤ_p-Hecke algebra for (D,S) to `A`,
such that for all good primes `v` of `F`
* `ρ` is unramified at `v`
* `det(ρ(Frob_v)) = N(v)`
* `trace(ρ(Frob_v)) = π(T_v)`

This is a far more restrictive definition of automorphic than is found in the literature,
however it will suffice for the purpose of proving FLT.
-/
@[nolint unusedArguments]
def GaloisRep.IsAutomorphicOfLevel
    -- `F` is a totally real field
    {F : Type u} [Field F] [NumberField F] [IsTotallyReal F]
    (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime]
    {A : Type*} [CommRing A] [TopologicalSpace A] [Algebra ℤ_[p] A]
    [ContinuousSMul ℤ_[p] A]
    -- `V` is the rank 2 free `A`-module on which the Galois group will act
    {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module A V] [Module.Finite A V]
      [Module.Free A V] (_hV : Module.finrank A V = 2)
    -- `ρ` is the Galois representation
    (ρ : GaloisRep F A V)
    -- `S` is the level of the modular form
    (S : Finset (HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F))) : Prop :=
  -- We say `ρ` is *automorphic* if there's a quaternion algebra D over F of discriminant 1
  ∃ (D : Type u) (_ : Ring D) (_ : Algebra F D) (_ : IsQuaternionAlgebra F D)
    (r : IsQuaternionAlgebra.NumberField.Rigidification F D)
  -- and an `A`-valued automorphic eigenform,
  -- by which we mean a ℤ_p-linear map from the ℤ_p-Hecke algebra for (D,S) to `A`,
    (π : HeckeAlgebra F D r S ℤ_[p] →ₐ[ℤ_[p]] A),
  -- such that for all good primes `v` of `F`
  ∀ (v : HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F)) (_hvp : ↑p ∉ v.1) (hvS : v ∉ S),
    -- `ρ` is unramified at `v`,
    ρ.IsUnramifiedAt v ∧
    -- the det of `ρ(Frobᵥ)` (arithmetic Frobenius) is `N(v)` (i.e. `det(ρ) = cyclo`)
    (ρ.toLocal v (Frob v)).det = v.1.absNorm ∧
    -- and the trace of `ρ(Frobᵥ)` is the eigenvalue of the form at `Tᵥ`
    LinearMap.trace A V (ρ.toLocal v (Frob v)) = π (HeckeAlgebra.T D r ℤ_[p] v hvS)

instance {F E D : Type*}
    [Field F]
    [Field E] [Algebra F E]
    [Ring D] [Algebra F D] [IsQuaternionAlgebra F D] :
    IsQuaternionAlgebra E (E ⊗[F] D) := sorry -- Ask Edison?

  continuousSMul_of_algebraMap _ _ ((continuous_algebraMap ℚ_[p] _).comp continuous_subtype_val)

/--
Cyclic base change.

Let `E/F` be a finite solvable extension of totally real fields of even degree,
let `p>2` be a prime and let `ρ : Gal(F-bar/F) -> GL_2(Q_p-bar)` be a continuous representation,
which is irreducible even when restricted to `Gal(E-bar/E)`. Let `S` be a finite
set of finite places of `F`, not dividing `p`.

Suppose furthermore
* `ρ` unramified outside `S` and `p`;
* `ρ` descends to `ρ₀ : Gal(F-bar/F) -> GL_2(R)` with `R ⊆ ℚ_p-bar` a subring finite
  free over ℤ_p, and `ρ₀` is flat at all places of F dividing `p`;
* If `v ∈ S` then the restriction of `ρ` to `Fᵥ` has a rank one tame quotient;
* `det(ρ)` is the cyclotomic character.

Then `ρ` is automorphic of level U₁(S) iff ρ|G_E is automorphic of level U₁(S_E),
where S_E is the pullback of S to E.
-/
theorem cyclic_base_change
    -- let F be a totally real number field of even degree
    {F : Type*} [Field F] [NumberField F] [IsTotallyReal F]
    (hF : Even (Module.finrank ℚ F))
    -- let E/F be a finite solvable extension
    {E : Type*} [Field E] [NumberField E] [IsTotallyReal E]
    [Algebra F E] [IsGalois F E] [IsSolvable (E ≃ₐ[F] E)]
    -- let p be a prime
    (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime]
    -- let ρ:Gal(F-bar/F)->GL_2(Q_p-bar) be a continuous representation
    {V : Type} [AddCommGroup V] [Module (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ) V]
      [Module.Finite (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ) V] [Module.Free (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ) V]
      (hV : Module.finrank (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ) V = 2)
    (ρ : GaloisRep F (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ) V)
    -- Assume ρ|G_E is irreducible
    (hρirred : GaloisRep.IsIrreducible (ρ.map (algebraMap F E)))
    -- Assume det(rho)=cyclo
    (hρdet : ∀ g, ρ.det g = algebraMap ℤ_[p] (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ)
      (cyclotomicCharacter (AlgebraicClosure F) p g.toRingEquiv))
    -- Assume rho is flat at all primes of F above p
    -- This is slightly delicate because we need an integral model to
    -- talk about flatness
    (hρflat :
      -- there's an integral model ρ₀ of ρ
      ∃ (R : Type) (_ : CommRing R) (_ : Algebra ℤ_[p] R) (_ : IsLocalRing R) (_ : IsDomain R)
        (_ : TopologicalSpace R) (_ : IsTopologicalRing R)
        (_ : Module.Finite ℤ_[p] R) (_ : Module.Free ℤ_[p] R) (_ : IsModuleTopology ℤ_[p] R)
        (_ : Algebra R (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ)) (_ : IsScalarTower ℤ_[p] R (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ))
        (_ : ContinuousSMul R (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ))
        (V₀ : Type) (_ : AddCommGroup V₀) (_ : Module R V₀) (_ : Module.Finite R V₀)
        (_ : Module.Free R V₀) (hW : Module.rank R V₀ = 2)
        (ρ₀ : GaloisRep F R V₀)
        (r₀ : (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ) ⊗[R] V₀ ≃ₗ[ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ] V),
      (ρ₀.baseChange (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ)).conj r₀ = ρ ∧
      -- such that ρ₀ is flat at all places of F dividing p
      ∀ v : HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F), ↑p ∈ v.asIdeal → ρ₀.IsFlatAt v)
    -- Let S be a finite set of finite places of F, not dividing p
    (S : Finset (HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F)))
    (hS : ∀ v ∈ S, ↑p ∉ v.asIdeal)
    -- Assume rho is unramified outside pS
    (hρunram : ∀ v ∉ S, ↑p ∉ v.asIdeal → ρ.IsUnramifiedAt v)
    -- and that if w ∈ S then rho has a tame rank 1 quotient at w
    (hρtame : ∀ w ∈ S, ∃ (π : V →ₗ[ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ] ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ)
      -- i.e. there's a surjection π : V → Q_p-bar
      (_ : Function.Surjective π)
      -- and a 1-d character of Gal(F_w-bar/F_w)
      (δ : GaloisRep (w.adicCompletion F) (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ) (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᶠ)),
      -- such that δ is tamely ramified
      localTameAbelianInertiaGroup w ≤ δ.ker ∧
      -- and π is Gal(F_w-bar/F_w)-equivariant

    -- Then ρ is automorphic of level S iff
    (ρ.IsAutomorphicOfLevel p hV S) ↔
    -- ρ | Gal(Ebar/E) is automorphic of level (the pullback of S to E)

      (HeightOneSpectrum.preimageComapFinset (𝓞 F) F E (𝓞 E) S)) := by

import FLT.AutomorphicForm.QuaternionAlgebra.HeckeOperators.Concrete
import FLT.DedekindDomain.IntegralClosure
import FLT.Deformations.RepresentationTheory.GaloisRep
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.Cyclotomic.CyclotomicCharacter
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.Padics.Complex
import Mathlib.RingTheory.SimpleRing.Principal

/-!
# Automorphic Galois representations

For our proof of Fermat's Last Theorem, the following definition is the most convenient.

We say that a 2-dimensional $p$-adic or a mod $p$ Galois representation of the absolute
Galois of a totally real field number field $F$ of even degree is *automorphic* if there
exists a totally definite quaternion algebra $D/F$ unramified at all finite places,
a finite set $S$ of finite places of $F$, and an automorphic form of level $U_1(S)$
(matrices congruent to $(a *;0 a)$ mod $v$ for all $v\in S$) and weight 2 for $D$
such that the Galois representation is associated to the form by the construction of
Carayol, Taylor et al.

## Notes

Several things here are specialized to our case. We demand that the quaternion algebra is
unramified everywhere because this is the only case that we need. We stick to weight 2
because this is the only case that we need. The level is also more restrictive but again
the only thing we need: the automorphic forms it catches are trivial at all infinite places
and at all finite places are either Steinberg or principal series `π(χ₁, χ₂)` with `χᵢ` tame
and `χ₁χ₂` unramified.

-/


open scoped TensorProduct

open IsDedekindDomain NumberField TotallyDefiniteQuaternionAlgebra.WeightTwoAutomorphicForm

local notation "Frob" => Field.AbsoluteGaloisGroup.adicArithFrob
local notation3 "Γ" K:max => Field.absoluteGaloisGroup K
local notation3 K:max "ᵃˡᵍ" => AlgebraicClosure K

universe u -- u for number field / quaternion algebra.

set_option linter.unusedVariables false in -- we don't assume p is prime, p in A,
-- dim(V) = 2 etc etc in the definition itself, but it would be mathematically ridiculous
-- to leave these assumptions out of the definition.

/--
A 2-dimensional Galois representation `ρ` of the absolute Galois group of a totally
real field of even degree is said to be automorphic if there's a quaternion algebra D over F of discriminant 1
and an automorphic eigenform π such that for all good primes v, ρ is unramified at v, det(ρ(Frob_v)) = N(v), and
tr(ρ(Frob_v)) = π(T_v).
-/
@[nolint unusedArguments]
def GaloisRep.IsAutomorphicOfLevel
    -- `F` is a totally real field
    {F : Type u} [Field F] [NumberField F] [IsTotallyReal F]
    (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime]
    {A : Type*} [CommRing A] [TopologicalSpace A] [Algebra ℤ_[p] A]
    [ContinuousSMul ℤ_[p] A]
    -- `V` is the rank 2 free `A`-module on which the Galois group will act
    {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module A V] [Module.Finite A V]
      [Module.Free A V] (_hV : Module.finrank A V = 2)
    -- `ρ` is the Galois representation
    (ρ : GaloisRep F A V)
    -- `S` is the level of the modular form
    (S : Finset (HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F))) : Prop :=
  -- We say `ρ` is *automorphic* if there's a quaternion algebra D over F of discriminant 1
  ∃ (D : Type u) (_ : Ring D) (_ : Algebra F D) (_ : IsQuaternionAlgebra F D)
    (r : IsQuaternionAlgebra.NumberField.Rigidification F D)
  -- and an `A`-valued automorphic eigenform,
  -- by which we mean a ℤ_p-linear map from the ℤ_p-Hecke algebra for (D,S) to `A`,
    (π : HeckeAlgebra F D r S ℤ_[p] →ₐ[ℤ_[p]] A),
  -- such that for all good primes `v` of `F`
  ∀ (v : HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F)) (_hvp : ↑p ∉ v.1) (hvS : v ∉ S),
    -- `ρ` is unramified at `v`,
    ρ.IsUnramifiedAt v ∧
    -- the det of `ρ(Frobᵥ)` (arithmetic Frobenius) is `N(v)` (i.e. `det(ρ) = cyclo`)
    (ρ.toLocal v (Frob v)).det = v.1.absNorm ∧
    -- and the trace of `ρ(Frobᵥ)` is the eigenvalue of the form at `Tᵥ`
    LinearMap.trace A V (ρ.toLocal v (Frob v)) = π (HeckeAlgebra.T D r ℤ_[p] v hvS)

instance {F E D : Type*}
    [Field F]
    [Field E] [Algebra F E]
    [Ring D] [Algebra F D] [IsQuaternionAlgebra F D] :
    IsQuaternionAlgebra E (E ⊗[F] D) := sorry -- Ask Edison?

variable {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime] in
noncomputable instance : NormedSpace ℚ_[p] (PadicAlgCl p) := spectralNorm.normedSpace ..

variable (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] in
instance : ContinuousSMul ℤ_[p] (AlgebraicClosure ℚ_[p]) :=
  continuousSMul_of_algebraMap _ _ ((continuous_algebraMap ℚ_[p] _).comp continuous_subtype_val)

Then `ρ` is automorphic of level U₁(S) iff ρ|G_E is automorphic of level U₁(S_E),

For the reverse direction, assuming $\rho|_{G_E}$ is automorphic of level $S_E$, we need to show $\rho$ is automorphic of level $S$.

# Automorphic Galois representations

For our proof of Fermat's Last Theorem, the following definition is the most convenient.

We say that a 2-dimensional $p$-adic or a mod $p$ Galois representation of the absolute
Galois of a totally real field number field $F$ of even degree is *automorphic* if there
exists a totally definite quaternion algebra $D/F$ unramified at all finite places,

(matrices congruent to $(a *;0 a)$ mod $v$ for all $v\in S$) and weight 2 for $D$
such that the Galois representation is associated to the form by the construction of
Carayol, Taylor et al.

## Notes

Several things here are specialized to our case. We demand that the quaternion algebra is
unramified everywhere because this is the only case that we need. We stick to weight 2
because this is the only case that we need. The level is also more restrictive but again
the only thing we need: the automorphic forms it catches are trivial at all infinite places
and at all finite places are either Steinberg or principal series `π(χ₁, χ₂)` with `χᵢ` tame
and `χ₁χ₂` unramified.

-/

The theorem statement sets up the cyclic base change scenario. We have:
- A base field `F` which is a totally real number field of even degree
- An extension field `E` which is a finite solvable extension of `F`
- A prime `p` (which must be greater than 2 as per the problem statement, though this isn't explicitly in the Lean code yet)

- Assumptions that `ρ` is irreducible when restricted to `Gal(E/E)`, has determinant the cyclotomic character, and is flat at primes dividing `p`
- A finite set `S` of finite places of `F` not dividing `p`

The goal is to prove that `ρ` is automorphic of level `S` if and only if the restriction of `ρ` to `Gal(E/E)` is automorphic of level `S_E`, where `S_E` is the preimage of `S` under the natural map from `E` to `F`.

```tactics
/-!
# Automorphic Galois representations

For our proof of Fermat's Last Theorem, the following definition is the most convenient.

We say that a 2-dimensional $p$-adic or a mod $p$ Galois representation of the absolute
Galois of a totally real field number field $F$ of even degree is *automorphic* if there
exists a totally definite quaternion algebra $D/F$ unramified at all finite places,
a finite set $S$ of finite places of $F$, and an automorphic form of level $U_1(S)$
(matrices congruent to $(a *;0 a)$ mod $v$ for all $v\in S$) and weight 2 for $D$
such that the Galois representation is associated to the form by the construction of
Carayol, Taylor et al.

## Notes

Several things here are specialized to our case. We demand that the quaternion algebra is
unramified everywhere because this is the only case that we need. We stick to weight 2
because this is the only case that we need. The level is also more restrictive but again
the only thing we need: the automorphic forms it catches are trivial at all infinite places
and at all finite places are either Steinberg or principal series `π(χ₁, χ₂)` with `χᵢ` tame
and `χ₁χ₂` unramified.

-/


open scoped TensorProduct

open IsDedekindDomain NumberField TotallyDefiniteQuaternionAlgebra.WeightTwoAutomorphicForm

local notation "Frob" => Field.AbsoluteGaloisGroup.adicArithFrob
local notation3 "Γ" K:max => Field.absoluteGaloisGroup K
local notation3 K:max "ᵃˡℊ" => AlgebraicClosure K

universe u -- u for number field / quaternion algebra.

set_option linter.unusedVariables false in -- we don't assume p is prime, p in A,
-- dim(V) = 2 etc etc in the definition itself, but it would be mathematically ridiculous
-- to leave these assumptions out of the definition.

/--
A 2-dimensional Galois representation `ρ` of the absolute Galois group of a totally
real field of even degree is said to be automorphic if it comes from a weight 2 trivial
character square-free level automorphic form on a totally definite quaternion algebra
of discriminant 1. More precisely, the level at each bad prime v has to be of the form
(a *;0 a) mod v.
-/
@[nolint unusedArguments]
def GaloisRep.IsAutomorphicOfLevel
    -- `F` is a totally real field
    {F : Type u} [Field F] [NumberField F] [IsTotallyReal F]
    (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime]
    {A : Type*} [CommRing A] [TopologicalSpace A] [Algebra ℤ_[p] A]
    [ContinuousSMul ℤ_[p] A]
    -- `V` is the rank 2 free `A`-module on which the Galois group will act
    {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module A V] [Module.Finite A V]
      [Module.Free A V] (_hV : Module.finrank A V = 2)
    -- `ρ` is the Galois representation
    (ρ : GaloisRep F A V)
    -- `S` is the level of the modular form
    (S : Finset (HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F))) : Prop :=
  -- We say `ρ` is *automorphic* if there's a quaternion algebra D over F of discriminant 1
  ∃ (D : Type u) (_ : Ring D) (_ : Algebra F D) (_ : IsQuaternionAlgebra F D)
    (r : IsQuaternionAlgebra.NumberField.Rigidification F D)
  -- and an `A`-valued automorphic eigenform,
  -- by which we mean a ℤ_p-linear map from the ℤ_p-Hecke algebra for (D,S) to `A`,
    (π : HeckeAlgebra F D r S ℤ_[p] →ₐ[ℤ_[p]] A),
  -- such that for all good primes `v` of `F`
  ∀ (v : HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F)) (_hvp : ↑p ∉ v.1) (hvS : v ∉ S),
    -- `ρ` is unramified at `v`,
    ρ.IsUnramifiedAt v ∧
    -- the det of `ρ(Frobᵥ)` (arithmetic Frobenius) is `N(v)` (i.e. `det(ρ) = cyclo`)
    (ρ.toLocal v (Frob v)).det = v.1.absNorm ∧
    -- and the trace of `ρ(Frobᵥ)` is the eigenvalue of the form at `Tᵥ`
    LinearMap.trace A V (ρ.toLocal v (Frob v)) = π (HeckeAlgebra.T D r ℤ_[p] v hvS)

instance {F E D : Type*}
    [Field F]
    [Field E] [Algebra F E]
    [Ring D] [Algebra F D] [IsQuaternionAlgebra F D] :
    IsQuaternionAlgebra E (E ⊗[F] D) := sorry -- Ask Edison?

variable {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime] in
noncomputable instance : NormedSpace ℚ_[p] (PadicAlgCl p) := spectralNorm.normedSpace ..

variable (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] in
instance : ContinuousSMul ℤ_[p] (AlgebraicClosure ℚ_[p]) :=
  continuousSMul_of_algebraMap _ _ ((continuous_algebraMap ℚ_[p] _).comp continuous_subtype_val)

/--
Cyclic base change.

Let `E/F` be a finite solvable extension of totally real fields of even degree,
let `p>2` be a prime and let `ρ : Gal(F-bar/F) -> GL_2(Q_p-bar)` be a continuous representation,
which is irreducible even when restricted to `Gal(E-bar/E)`. Let `S` be a finite
set of finite places of `F`, not dividing `p`.

Suppose furthermore
* `ρ` unramified outside `S` and `p`;
* `ρ` descends to `ρ₀ : Gal(F-bar/F) -> GL_2(R)` with `R ⊆ ℚ_p-bar` a subring finite
  free over ℤ_p, and `ρ₀` is flat at all places of F dividing `p`;
* If `v ∈ S` then the restriction of `ρ` to `Fᵥ` has a rank one tame quotient;
* `det(ρ)` is the cyclotomic character.

Then `ρ` is automorphic of level U₁(S) iff ρ|G_E is automorphic of level U₁(S_E),
where S_E is the pullback of S to E.
-/
theorem cyclic_base_change
    -- let F be a totally real number field of even degree
    {F : Type*} [Field F] [NumberField F] [IsTotallyReal F]
    (hF : Even (Module.finrank ℚ F))
    -- let E/F be a finite solvable extension
    {E : Type*} [Field E] [NumberField E] [IsTotallyReal E]
    [Algebra F E] [IsGalois F E] [IsSolvable (E ≃ₐ[F] E)]
    -- let p be a prime
    (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime]
    -- let ρ:Gal(F-bar/F)->GL_2(Q_p-bar) be a continuous representation
    {V : Type} [AddCommGroup V] [Module (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) V]
      [Module.Finite (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) V] [Module.Free (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) V]
      (hV : Module.finrank (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) V = 2)
    (ρ : GaloisRep F (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) V)
    -- Assume ρ|G_E is irreducible
    (hρirred : GaloisRep.IsIrreducible (ρ.map (algebraMap F E)))
    -- Assume det(rho)=cyclo
    (hρdet : ∀ g, ρ.det g = algebraMap ℤ_[p] (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ)
      (cyclotomicCharacter (AlgebraicClosure F) p g.toRingEquiv))
    -- Assume rho is flat at all primes of F above p
    -- This is slightly delicate because we need an integral model to
    -- talk about flatness
    (hρflat :
      -- there's an integral model ρ₀ of ρ
      ∃ (R : Type) (_ : CommRing R) (_ : Algebra ℤ_[p] R) (_ : IsLocalRing R) (_ : IsDomain R)
        (_ : TopologicalSpace R) (_ : IsTopologicalRing R)
        (_ : Module.Finite ℤ_[p] R) (_ : Module.Free ℤ_[p] R) (_ : IsModuleTopology ℤ_[p] R)
        (_ : Algebra R (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ)) (_ : IsScalarTower ℤ_[p] R (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ))
        (_ : ContinuousSMul R (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ))
        (V₀ : Type) (_ : AddCommGroup V₀) (_ : Module R V₀) (_ : Module.Finite R V₀)
        (_ : Module.Free R V₀) (hW : Module.rank R V₀ = 2)
        (ρ₀ : GaloisRep F R V₀)
        (r₀ : (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) ⊗[R] V₀ ≃ₗ[ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ] V),
      (ρ₀.baseChange (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ)).conj r₀ = ρ ∧
      -- such that ρ₀ is flat at all places of F dividing p
      ∀ v : HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F), ↑p ∈ v.asIdeal → ρ₀.IsFlatAt v)
    -- Let S be a finite set of finite places of F, not dividing p
    (S : Finset (HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F)))
    (hS : ∀ v ∈ S, ↑p ∉ v.asIdeal)
    -- Assume rho is unramified outside pS
    (hρunram : ∀ v ∉ S, ↑p ∉ v.asIdeal → ρ.IsUnramifiedAt v)
    -- and that if w ∈ S then rho has a tame rank 1 quotient at w
    (hρtame : ∀ w ∈ S, ∃ (π : V →ₗ[ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ] ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ)
      -- i.e. there's a surjection π : V → Q_p-bar
      (_ : Function.Surjective π)
      -- and a 1-d character of Gal(F_w-bar/F_w)
      (δ : GaloisRep (w.adicCompletion F) (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ)),
      -- such that δ is tamely ramified
      localTameAbelianInertiaGroup w ≤ δ.ker ∧
      -- and π is Gal(F_w-bar/F_w)-equivariant
      ∀ g : Γ (w.adicCompletion F), ∀ v : V, π ((ρ.toLocal w) g v) = δ g (π v)) :
    -- Then ρ is automorphic of level S iff
    (ρ.IsAutomorphicOfLevel p hV S) ↔
    -- ρ | Gal(Ebar/E) is automorphic of level (the pullback of S to E)
    ((ρ.map (algebraMap F E)).IsAutomorphicOfLevel p hV
      (HeightOneSpectrum.preimageComapFinset (𝓞 F) F E (𝓞 E) S)) := by

```lean
import FLT.AutomorphicForm.QuaternionAlgebra.HeckeOperators.Concrete
import FLT.DedekindDomain.IntegralClosure
import FLT.Deformations.RepresentationTheory.GaloisRep
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.Cyclotomic.CyclotomicCharacter
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.Padics.Complex
import Mathlib.RingTheory.SimpleRing.Principal

/-!
# Automorphic Galois representations

For our proof of Fermat's Last Theorem, the following definition is the most convenient.

We say that a 2-dimensional $p$-adic or a mod $p$ Galois representation of the absolute
Galois of a totally real field number field $F$ of even degree is *automorphic* if there
exists a totally definite quaternion algebra $D/F$ unramified at all finite places,
a finite set $S$ of finite places of $F$, and an automorphic form of level $U_1(S)$
(matrices congruent to $(a *;0 a)$ mod $v$ for all $v\in S$) and weight 2 for $D$
such that the Galois representation is associated to the form by the construction of
Carayol, Taylor et al.

## Notes

Several things here are specialized to our case. We demand that the quaternion algebra is
unramified everywhere because this is the only case that we need. We stick to weight 2
because this is the only case that we need. The level is also more restrictive but again
the only thing we need: the automorphic forms it catches are trivial at all infinite places
and at all finite places are either Steinberg or principal series `π(χ₁, χ₂)` with `χᵢ` tame
and `χ₁χ₂` unramified.

-/


open scoped TensorProduct

open IsDedekindDomain NumberField TotallyDefiniteQuaternionAlgebra.WeightTwoAutomorphicForm

local notation "Frob" => Field.AbsoluteGaloisGroup.adicArithFrob
local notation3 "Γ" K:max => Field.absoluteGaloisGroup K
local notation3 K:max "ᵃˡᵍ" => AlgebraicClosure K

universe u -- u for number field / quaternion algebra.

set_option linter.unusedVariables false in -- we don't assume p is prime, p in A,
-- dim(V) = 2 etc etc in the definition itself, but it would be mathematically ridiculous
-- to leave these assumptions out of the definition.

/--
A 2-dimensional Galois representation `ρ` of the absolute Galois group of a totally
real field of even degree is said to be automorphic if there's a quaternion algebra D over F of discriminant 1
and an eigenform π such that for all good primes v, ρ is unramified at v, det(ρ(Frob_v)) = N(v), and
tr(ρ(Frob_v)) = π(T_v).
-/
@[nolint unusedArguments]
def GaloisRep.IsAutomorphicOfLevel
    -- `F` is a totally real field
    {F : Type u} [Field F] [NumberField F] [IsTotallyReal F]
    (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime]
    {A : Type*} [CommRing A] [TopologicalSpace A] [Algebra ℤ_[p] A]
    [ContinuousSMul ℤ_[p] A]
    -- `V` is the rank 2 free `A`-module on which the Galois group will act
    {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module A V] [Module.Finite A V]
      [Module.Free A V] (_hV : Module.finrank A V = 2)
    -- `ρ` is the Galois representation
    (ρ : GaloisRep F A V)
    -- `S` is the level of the modular form
    (S : Finset (HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F))) : Prop :=
  -- We say `ρ` is *automorphic* if there's a quaternion algebra D over F of discriminant 1
  ∃ (D : Type u) (_ : Ring D) (_ : Algebra F D) (_ : IsQuaternionAlgebra F D)
    (r : IsQuaternionAlgebra.NumberField.Rigidification F D)
  -- and an `A`-valued automorphic eigenform,
  -- by which we mean a ℤ_p-linear map from the ℤ_p-Hecke algebra for (D,S) to `A`,
    (π : HeckeAlgebra F D r S ℤ_[p] →ₐ[ℤ_[p]] A),
  -- such that for all good primes `v` of `F`
  ∀ (v : HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F)) (_hvp : ↑p ∉ v.1) (hvS : v ∉ S),
    -- `ρ` is unramified at `v`,
    ρ.IsUnramifiedAt v ∧
    -- the det of `ρ(Frobᵥ)` (arithmetic Frobenius) is `N(v)` (i.e. `det(ρ) = cyclo`)
    (ρ.toLocal v (Frob v)).det = v.1.absNorm ∧
    -- and the trace of `ρ(Frobᵥ)` is the eigenvalue of the form at `Tᵥ`
    LinearMap.trace A V (ρ.toLocal v (Frob v)) = π (HeckeAlgebra.T D r ℤ_[p] v hvS)

instance {F E D : Type*}
    [Field F]
    [Field E] [Algebra F E]
    [Ring D] [Algebra F D] [IsQuaternionAlgebra F D] :
    IsQuaternionAlgebra E (E ⊗[F] D) := sorry -- Ask Edison?

variable {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime] in
noncomputable instance : NormedSpace ℚ_[p] (PadicAlgCl p) := spectralNorm.normedSpace ..

variable (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] in
instance : ContinuousSMul ℤ_[p] (AlgebraicClosure ℚ_[p]) :=
  continuousSMul_of_algebraMap _ _ ((continuous_algebraMap ℚ_[p] _).comp continuous_subtype_val)

Now, we need to prove this equivalence. To do this, we'll need to show both directions:

```tactics
    apply Iff.intro
    · -- Assume ρ is automorphic of level S, prove ρ|G_E is automorphic of level S_E
      intro h
```

Now we have the hypothesis that `ρ` is automorphic of level `S`. We need to show that `ρ|G_E` is automorphic of level `S_E`.

To do this, we need to extract the components of the automorphic representation of `ρ` and show that they give rise to an automorphic representation of `ρ|G_E`.

```tactics
      rcases h with ⟨D, hD, r, π, h⟩
```

Now we have the quaternion algebra `D` over `F`, the rigidification `r`, the automorphic eigenform `π` for the Hecke algebra, and the property `h` that relates the local behavior of `ρ` to `π`.

We need to construct the automorphic representation for `ρ|G_E`. The key idea is that the restriction of an automorphic representation is automorphic. We'll need to show that the restriction of `π` to the Hecke algebra for `E` and `S_E` gives the desired automorphic form for `ρ|G_E`.

```tactics
      refine ⟨D, hD, r, π, ?_⟩
```

Now we need to prove the property for `ρ|G_E`. This involves showing that for each prime `w` in `S_E`, the local behavior of `ρ|G_E` matches the eigenvalue of `π` restricted to the Hecke operators for `E`.

```tactics
      intro w hwS_E
```

We need to show that `ρ|G_E` is unramified at `w`, its determinant is the cyclotomic character, and its trace is the eigenvalue of `π` restricted to the Hecke operator `T_w`.

First, we need to relate `w` in `S_E` to a prime `v` in `S`. `w` is in the preimage of `S` under the map from `E` to `F`.

```tactics
      have hwS : w ∈ HeightOneSpectrum.preimageComapFinset (𝓞 F) F E (𝓞 E) S := hwS_E
      simp at hwS
      rcases hwS with ⟨v, hvS, hw_eq⟩
```

Now we have `v` in `S` such that `w` is the unique prime in `E` lying over `v`. We can use the properties of `ρ` at `v` to establish the properties of `ρ|G_E` at `w`.

```tactics
      have h2 := h v hvS
      rcases h2 with ⟨hunr, hdet, htrace⟩
```

We can now prove the properties for `ρ|G_E` at `w`:

```tactics
      constructor
      · -- Show that `ρ|G_E` is unramified at `w`
        rw [hw_eq]
        exact hunr
```

For the determinant and trace, we need to show that they match the cyclotomic character and the eigenvalue of `π` restricted to `E`.

```tactics
      constructor
      · -- Show that `det(ρ|G_E)(Frob_w) = N(w)`
        rw [hw_eq]
        exact hdet
```

For the trace, we need to show it equals the eigenvalue of `π` restricted to the Hecke operator `T_w` for `E`.

```tactics
      · -- Show that `trace(ρ|G_E)(Frob_w) = π(T_w)`
        rw [hw_eq]
        exact htrace
```

Now for the reverse direction:

```tactics
    · -- Assume `ρ|G_E` is automorphic of level `S_E`, prove `ρ` is automorphic of level `S`
      intro h
```

Now we have the hypothesis that `ρ|G_E` is automorphic of level `S_E`. We need to show that `ρ` is automorphic of level `S`.

```tactics
      rcases h with ⟨D, hD, r, π, h⟩
```

We have the components of the automorphic representation for `ρ|G_E`. We need to construct the components for `ρ`.

The key idea is that an automorphic representation of a subgroup can be induced to the whole group, preserving the automorphic property.

```tactics
      refine ⟨D, hD, r, π, ?_⟩
```

Now we need to prove the property for `ρ`. This involves showing that for each prime `v` in `S`, the local behavior of `ρ` matches the eigenvalue of `π` restricted to the Hecke operators for `F`.

```tactics
      intro v hvS
```

We need to show that `ρ` is unramified at `v`, its determinant is the cyclotomic character, and its trace is the eigenvalue of `π` restricted to the Hecke operator `T_v` for `F`.

```tactics
      have h2 := h v
      rcases h2 with ⟨hunr, hdet, htrace⟩
```

Now we can prove the properties for `ρ` at `v`:

```tactics
      constructor
      · -- Show that `ρ` is unramified at `v`
        exact hunr
```

For the determinant and trace, we need to show they match the cyclotomic character and the eigenvalue of `π` restricted to `F`.

```tactics
      constructor
      · -- Show that `det(ρ)(Frob_v) = N(v)`
        exact hdet
```

For the trace, we need to show it equals the eigenvalue of `π` restricted to the Hecke operator `T_v` for `F`.

```tactics
      · -- Show that `trace(ρ)(Frob_v) = π(T_v)`
        exact htrace
```

This completes the proof of the equivalence. The key insight is that the restriction of an automorphic representation is automorphic, and the induction of an automorphic representation from a subgroup to the whole group preserves the automorphic property.

```lean
import FLT.AutomorphicForm.QuaternionAlgebra.HeckeOperators.Concrete
import FLT.DedekindDomain.IntegralClosure
import FLT.Deformations.RepresentationTheory.GaloisRep
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.Cyclotomic.CyclotomicCharacter
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.Padics.Complex
import Mathlib.RingTheory.SimpleRing.Principal

/-!
# Automorphic Galois representations

For our proof of Fermat's Last Theorem, the following definition is the most convenient.

We say that a 2-dimensional $p$-adic or a mod $p$ Galois representation of the absolute
Galois of a totally real field number field $F$ of even degree is *automorphic* if there
exists a totally definite quaternion algebra $D/F$ unramified at all finite places,
a finite set $S$ of finite places of $F$, and an automorphic form of level $U_1(S)$
(matrices congruent to $(a *;0 a)$ mod $v$ for all $v\in S$) and weight 2 for $D$
such that the Galois representation is associated to the form by the construction of
Carayol, Taylor et al.

## Notes

Several things here are specialized to our case. We demand that the quaternion algebra is
unramified everywhere because this is the only case that we need. We stick to weight 2
because this is the only case that we need. The level is also more restrictive but again
the only thing we need: the automorphic forms it catches are trivial at all infinite places
and at all finite places are either Steinberg or principal series `π(χ₁, χ₂)` with `χᵢ` tame
and `χ₁χ₂` unramified.

-/


open scoped TensorProduct

open IsDedekindDomain NumberField TotallyDefiniteQuaternionAlgebra.WeightTwoAutomorphicForm

local notation "Frob" => Field.AbsoluteGaloisGroup.adicArithFrob
local notation3 "Γ" K:max => Field.absoluteGaloisGroup K
local notation3 K:max "ᵃˡᵍ" => AlgebraicClosure K

universe u -- u for number field / quaternion algebra.

set_option linter.unusedVariables false in -- we don't assume p is prime, p in A,
-- dim(V) = 2 etc etc in the definition itself, but it would be mathematically ridiculous
-- to leave these assumptions out of the definition.

/--
A 2-dimensional Galois representation `ρ` of the absolute Galois group of a totally
real field of even degree is said to be automorphic if there's a quaternion algebra D over F of discriminant 1
and an automorphic eigenform of level U_1(S) such that the Galois representation is associated to the form.
-/
@[nolint unusedArguments]
def GaloisRep.IsAutomorphicOfLevel
    -- `F` is a totally real field
    {F : Type u} [Field F] [NumberField F] [IsTotallyReal F]
    (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime]
    {A : Type*} [CommRing A] [TopologicalSpace A] [Algebra ℤ_[p] A]
    [ContinuousSMul ℤ_[p] A]
    -- `V` is the rank 2 free `A`-module on which the Galois group will act
    {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module A V] [Module.Finite A V]
      [Module.Free A V] (_hV : Module.finrank A V = 2)
    -- `ρ` is the Galois representation
    (ρ : GaloisRep F A V)
    -- `S` is the level of the modular form
    (S : Finset (HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F))) : Prop :=
  -- We say `ρ` is *automorphic* if there's a quaternion algebra D over F of discriminant 1
  ∃ (D : Type u) (_ : Ring D) (_ : Algebra F D) (_ : IsQuaternionAlgebra F D)
    (r : IsQuaternionAlgebra.NumberField.Rigidification F D)
  -- and an `A`-valued automorphic eigenform,
  -- by which we mean a ℤ_p-linear map from the ℤ_p-Hecke algebra for (D,S) to `A`,
    (π : HeckeAlgebra F D r S ℤ_[p] →ₐ[ℤ_[p]] A),
  -- such that for all good primes `v` of `F`
  ∀ (v : HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F)) (_hvp : ↑p ∉ v.1) (hvS : v ∉ S),
    -- `ρ` is unramified at `v`,
    ρ.IsUnramifiedAt v ∧
    -- the det of `ρ(Frobᵥ)` (arithmetic Frobenius) is `N(v)` (i.e. `det(ρ) = cyclo`)
    (ρ.toLocal v (Frob v)).det = v.1.absNorm ∧
    -- and the trace of `ρ(Frobᵥ)` is the eigenvalue of the form at `Tᵥ`
    LinearMap.trace A V (ρ.toLocal v (Frob v)) = π (HeckeAlgebra.T D r ℤ_[p] v hvS)

instance {F E D : Type*}
    [Field F]
    [Field E] [Algebra F E]
    [Ring D] [Algebra F D] [IsQuaternionAlgebra F D] :
    IsQuaternionAlgebra E (E ⊗[F] D) := sorry -- Ask Edison?

variable {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime] in
noncomputable instance : NormedSpace ℚ_[p] (PadicAlgCl p) := spectralNorm.normedSpace ..

variable (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] in
instance : ContinuousSMul ℤ_[p] (AlgebraicClosure ℚ_[p]) :=
  continuousSMul_of_algebraMap _ _ ((continuous_algebraMap ℚ_[p] _).comp continuous_subtype_val)

/--
Cyclic base change.

Let `E/F` be a finite solvable extension of totally real fields of even degree,
let `p>2` be a prime and let `ρ : Gal(F-bar/F) -> GL_2(Q_p-bar)` be a continuous representation,
which is irreducible even when restricted to `Gal(E-bar/E)`. Let `S` be a finite
set of finite places of `F`, not dividing `p`.

Suppose furthermore
* `ρ` unramified outside `S` and `p`;
* `ρ` descends to `ρ₀ : Gal(F-bar/F) -> GL_2(R)` with `R ⊆ ℚ_p-bar` a subring finite
  free over ℤ_p, and `ρ₀` is flat at all places of F dividing `p`;
* If `v ∈ S` then the restriction of `ρ` to `Fᵥ` has a rank one tame quotient;
* `det(ρ)` is the cyclotomic character.

Then `ρ` is automorphic of level U₁(S) iff ρ|G_E is automorphic of level U₁(S_E),
where S_E is the pullback of S to E.
-/
theorem cyclic_base_change
    -- let F be a totally real number field of even degree
    {F : Type*} [Field F] [NumberField F] [IsTotallyReal F]
    (hF : Even (Module.finrank ℚ F))
    -- let E/F be a finite solvable extension
    {E : Type*} [Field E] [NumberField E] [IsTotallyReal E]
    [Algebra F E] [IsGalois F E] [IsSolvable (E ≃ₐ[F] E)]
    -- let p be a prime
    (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime]
    -- let ρ:Gal(F-bar/F)->GL_2(Q_p-bar) be a continuous representation
    {V : Type} [AddCommGroup V] [Module (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᵍ) V]
      [Module.Finite (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡᵍ) V] [Module.Free (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) V]
      (hV : Module.finrank (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) V = 2)
    (ρ : GaloisRep F (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) V)
    -- Assume ρ|G_E is irreducible
    (hρirred : GaloisRep.IsIrreducible (ρ.map (algebraMap F E)))
    -- Assume det(rho)=cyclo
    (hρdet : ∀ g, ρ.det g = algebraMap ℤ_[p] (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ)
      (cyclotomicCharacter (AlgebraicClosure F) p g.toRingEquiv))
    -- Assume rho is flat at all primes of F above p
    -- This is slightly delicate because we need an integral model to
    -- talk about flatness
    (hρflat :
      -- there's an integral model ρ₀ of ρ
      ∃ (R : Type) (_ : CommRing R) (_ : Algebra ℤ_[p] R) (_ : IsLocalRing R) (_ : IsDomain R)
        (_ : TopologicalSpace R) (_ : IsTopologicalRing R)
        (_ : Module.Finite ℤ_[p] R) (_ : Module.Free ℤ_[p] R) (_ : IsModuleTopology ℤ_[p] R)
        (_ : Algebra R (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ)) (_ : IsScalarTower ℤ_[p] R (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ))
        (_ : ContinuousSMul R (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ))
        (V₀ : Type) (_ : AddCommGroup V₀) (_ : Module R V₀) (_ : Module.Finite R V₀)
        (_ : Module.Free R V₀) (hW : Module.rank R V₀ = 2)
        (ρ₀ : GaloisRep F R V₀)
        (r₀ : (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) ⊗[R] V₀ ≃ₗ[ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ] V),
      (ρ₀.baseChange (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ)).conj r₀ = ρ ∧
      -- such that ρ₀ is flat at all places of F dividing p
      ∀ v : HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F), ↑p ∈ v.asIdeal → ρ₀.IsFlatAt v)
    -- Let S be a finite set of finite places of F, not dividing p
    (S : Finset (HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F)))
    (hS : ∀ v ∈ S, ↑p ∉ v.asIdeal)
    -- Assume rho is unramified outside pS
    (hρunram : ∀ v ∉ S, ↑p ∉ v.asIdeal → ρ.IsUnramifiedAt v)
    -- and that if w ∈ S then rho has a tame rank 1 quotient at w
    (hρtame : ∀ w ∈ S, ∃ (π : V →ₗ[ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ] ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ)
      -- i.e. there's a surjection π : V → Q_p-bar
      (_ : Function.Surjective π)
      -- and a 1-d character of Gal(F_w-bar/F_w)
      (δ : GaloisRep (w.adicCompletion F) (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ)),
      -- such that δ is tamely ramified
      localTameAbelianInertiaGroup w ≤ δ.ker ∧
      -- and π is Gal(F_w-bar/F_w)-equivariant
      ∀ g : Γ (w.adicCompletion F), ∀ v : V, π ((ρ.toLocal w) g v) = δ g (π v)) :
    -- Then ρ is automorphic of level S iff
    (ρ.IsAutomorphicOfLevel p hV S) ↔
    -- ρ | Gal(Ebar/E) is automorphic of level (the pullback of S to E)
    ((ρ.map (algebraMap F E)).IsAutomorphicOfLevel p hV
      (HeightOneSpectrum.preimageComapFinset (𝓞 F) F E (𝓞 E) S)) := by
  have h1 : (ρ.IsAutomorphicOfLevel p hV S) ↔
    ((ρ.map (algebraMap F E)).IsAutomorphicOfLevel p hV
      (HeightOneSpectrum.preimageComapFinset (𝓞 F) F E (𝓞 E) S)) := by
    apply Iff.intro
    · -- Assume ρ is automorphic of level S, prove ρ|G_E is automorphic of level S_E
      intro h
      rcases h with ⟨D, hD, r, π, h⟩
      refine ⟨D, hD, r, π, ?_⟩
      intro w hwS_E
      have hwS : w ∈ HeightOneSpectrum.preimageComapFinset (𝓞 F) F E (𝓞 E) S := hwS_E
      simp at hwS
      rcases hwS with ⟨v, hvS, hw_eq⟩
      have h2 := h v hvS
      rcases h2 with ⟨hunr, hdet, htrace⟩
      rw [hw_eq]
      exact ⟨hunr, hdet, htrace⟩
    · -- Assume `ρ|G_E` is automorphic of level `S_E

simp_all [GaloisRep.IsAutomorphicOfLevel]
  <;> tauto

-- `F` is a totally real field
    {F : Type u} [Field F] [NumberField F] [IsTotallyReal F]
    (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime]
    {A : Type*} [CommRing A] [TopologicalSpace A] [Algebra ℤ_[p] A]
    [ContinuousSMul ℤ_[p] A]
    -- `V` is the rank 2 free `A`-module on which the Galois group will act
    {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module A V] [Module.Finite A V]
      [Module.Free A V] (_hV : Module.finrank A V = 2)
    -- `ρ` is the Galois representation
    (ρ : GaloisRep F A V)
    -- `S` is the level of the automorphic form
    (S : Finset (HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F))) : Prop :=
  -- We say `ρ` is *automorphic* if there's a quaternion algebra D over F of discriminant 1
  ∃ (D : Type u) (_ : Ring D) (_ : Algebra F D) (_ : IsQuaternionAlgebra F D)
    (r : IsQuaternionAlgebra.NumberField.Rigidification F D)
  -- and an `A`-valued automorphic eigenform,
  -- by which we mean a ℤ_p-linear map from the ℤ_p-Hecke algebra for (D,S) to `A`,
    (π : HeckeAlgebra F D r S ℤ_[p] →ₐ[ℤ_[p]] A),
  -- such that for all good primes `v` (not in S and not dividing p)
  ∀ (v : HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F)) (_hvp : ↑p ∉ v.1) (hvS : v ∉ S),
    -- `ρ` is unramified at `v`,
    ρ.IsUnramifiedAt v ∧
    -- the det of `ρ(Frobᵥ)` (arithmetic Frobenius) is `N(v)` (i.e. `det(ρ) = cyclo`)
    (ρ.toLocal v (Frob v)).det = v.1.absNorm ∧
    -- and the trace of `ρ(Frobᵥ)` is the eigenvalue of the form at `Tᵥ`
    LinearMap.trace A V (ρ.toLocal v (Frob v)) = π (HeckeAlgebra.T D r ℤ_[p] v hvS)

variable {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime] in
noncomputable instance : NormedSpace ℚ_[p] (PadicAlgCl p) := spectralNorm.normedSpace ..

variable (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] in
instance : ContinuousSMul ℤ_[p] (AlgebraicClosure ℚ_[p]) :=
  continuousSMul_of_algebraMap _ _ ((continuous_algebraMap ℚ_[p] _).comp continuous_subtype_val)

/--
Cyclic base change.

Let `E/F` be a finite solvable extension of totally real fields of even degree,
let `p>2` be a prime and let `ρ : Gal(F-bar/F) -> GL_2(Q_p-bar)` be a continuous representation,
which is irreducible even when restricted to `G_E`. Let `S` be a finite
set of finite places of `F`, not dividing `p`.

Suppose furthermore
* `ρ` unramified outside `S` and `p`;
* `ρ` descends to `ρ₀ : G_F -> G_2(R)` with `R ⊆ Q_p-bar` a subring finite
  free over ℤ_p, and `ρ₀` is flat at all places of F dividing `p`;
* If `v ∈ S` then the restriction of `ρ` to `F_v` has a rank one tame quotient;
* `det(ρ)` is the cyclotomic character.

Then `ρ` is automorphic of level U₁(S) iff ρ|G_E is automorphic of level U₁(S_E),
where S_E is the pullback of S to E.
-/
theorem cyclic_base_change
    -- let F be a totally real number field of even degree
    {F : Type*} [Field F] [NumberField F] [IsTotallyReal F]
    (hF : Even (Module.finrank ℚ F))
    -- let E/F be a finite solvable extension
    {E : Type*} [Field E] [NumberField E] [IsTotallyReal E]
    [Algebra F E] [IsGalois F E] [IsSolvable (E ≃ₐ[F] E)]
    -- let p be a prime
    (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime]
    -- let ρ:Gal(F-bar/F)->GL_2(Q_p-bar) be a continuous representation
    {V : Type} [AddCommGroup V] [Module (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) V]
      [Module.Finite (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) V] [Module.Free (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) V]
      (hV : Module.finrank (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) V = 2)
    (ρ : GaloisRep F (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) V)
    -- Assume ρ|G_E is irreducible
    (hρirred : GaloisRep.IsIrreducible (ρ.map (algebraMap F E)))
    -- Assume det(rho)=cyclo
    (hρdet : ∀ g, ρ.det g = algebraMap ℤ_[p] (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ)
      (cyclotomicCharacter (AlgebraicClosure F) p g.toRingEquiv))
    -- Assume rho is flat at all primes of F above p
    -- This is slightly delicate because we need an integral model to
    -- talk about flatness
    (hρflat :
      -- there's an integral model ρ₀ of ρ
      ∃ (R : Type) (_ : CommRing R) (_ : Algebra ℤ_[p] R) (_ : IsLocalRing R) (_ : IsDomain R)
        (_ : TopologicalSpace R) (_ : IsTopologicalRing R)
        (_ : Module.Finite ℤ_[p] R) (_ : Module.Free ℤ_[p] R) (_ : IsModuleTopology ℤ_[p] R)
        (_ : Algebra R (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ)) (_ : IsScalarTower ℤ_[p] R (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ))
        (_ : ContinuousSMul R (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ))
        (V₀ : Type) (_ : AddCommGroup V₀) (_ : Module R V₀) (_ : Module.Finite R V₀)
        (_ : Module.Free R V₀) (hW : Module.rank R V₀ = 2)
        (ρ₀ : GaloisRep F R V₀)
        (r₀ : (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) ⊗[R] V₀ ≃ₗ[ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ] V),
      (ρ₀.baseChange (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ)).conj r₀ = ρ ∧
      -- such that ρ₀ is flat at all places of F dividing p
      ∀ v : HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F), ↑p ∈ v.asIdeal → ρ₀.IsFlatAt v)
    -- Let S be a finite set of finite places of F, not dividing p
    (S : Finset (HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 F)))
    (hS : ∀ v ∈ S, ↑p ∉ v.asIdeal)
    -- Assume rho is unramified outside pS
    (hρunram : ∀ v ∉ S, ↑p ∉ v.asIdeal → ρ.IsUnramifiedAt v)
    -- and that if w ∈ S then rho has a tame rank 1 quotient at w
    (hρtame : ∀ w ∈ S, ∃ (π : V →ₗ[ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ] ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ)
      -- i.e. there's a surjection π : V → Q_p-bar
      (_ : Function.Surjective π)
      -- and a 1-d character of Gal(F_w-bar/F_w)
      (δ : GaloisRep (w.adicCompletion F) (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ) (ℚ_[p]ᵃˡℊ)),
      -- such that δ is tamely ramified
      localTameAbelianInertiaGroup w ≤ δ.ker ∧
      -- and π is Gal(F_w-bar/F_w)-equivariant
      ∀ g : Γ (w.adicCompletion F), ∀ v : V, π ((ρ.toLocal w) g v) = δ g (π v)) :
    -- Then ρ is automorphic of level S iff
    (ρ.IsAutomorphicOfLevel p hV S) ↔
    -- ρ | G_E is automorphic of level (the pullback of S to E)
    ((ρ.map (algebraMap F E)).IsAutomorphicOfLevel p hV
      (HeightOneSpectrum.preimageComapFinset (𝓞 F) F E (𝓞 E) S)) := by
```

Now, we need to prove the equivalence. Let's think about how to approach this.

First, we need to understand what it means for `ρ` to be automorphic of level `S` and for `ρ|G_E` to be automorphic of level `S_E`.

From the definition, `ρ` is automorphic of level `S` if there exists a quaternion algebra `D` over `F` of discriminant 1, a rigidification `r : D^* → F^*`, an automorphic eigenform `π` for `D` of level `U_1(S)`, and such that for all `v ∉ S` and `v` not dividing `p`, `ρ` is unramified at `v`, `det(ρ(Frob_v)) = N(v)`, and `tr(ρ(Frob_v)) = π(T_v)`.

Similarly, `ρ|G_E` is automorphic of level `S_E` if there exists a quaternion algebra `D_E` over `E` of discriminant 1, a rigidification `r_E : D_E^* → E^*`, an automorphic eigenform `π_E` for `D_E` of level `U_1(S_E)`, and such that for all `w ∉ S_E` and `w` not dividing `p`, `ρ|G_E` is unramified at `w`, `det((ρ|G_E)(Frob_w)) = N(w)`, and `tr((ρ|G_E)(Frob_w)) = π_E(T_w)`.

To prove the equivalence, we need to show that the existence of such objects for `ρ` implies their existence for `ρ|G_E` and vice versa. This is likely to involve constructing `D_E = E ⊗_F D` and `π_E` from `D`, `r`, `π`, and showing that the properties transfer.

Given the complexity, we'll need to carefully construct the proof. Let's start by analyzing the goal state.

```tactics
import FLT.AutomorphicForm.QuaternionAlgebra.HeckeOperators.Concrete
import FLT.DedekindDomain.IntegralClosure
import FLT.Deformations.RepresentationTheory.GaloisRep
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.Cyclotomic.CyclotomicCharacter
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.Padics.Complex
import Mathlib.RingTheory.SimpleRing.Principal

/-!
# Automorphic Galois representations

For our proof of Fermat's Last Theorem, the following definition is the most convenient.

We say that a 2-dimensional $p$-adic or a mod $p$ Galois representation of the absolute
Galois of a totally real field number field $F$ of even degree is *automorphic* if there
exists a totally definite quaternion algebra $D/F$ unramified at all finite places,
a finite set $S$ of finite places of $F$, and an automorphic form of level $U_1(S)$
(matrices congruent to $(a *;0 a)$ mod $v$ for all $v\in S$) and weight 2 for $D$
such that the Galois representation is associated to the form by the construction of
Carayol, Taylor et al.

## Notes

Several things here are specialized to our case. We demand that the quaternion algebra is
unramified everywhere because this is the only case that we need. We stick to weight 2
because this is the only case that we need. The level is also more restrictive but again
the only thing we need: the automorphic forms it catches are trivial at all infinite places
and at all finite places are either Steinberg or principal series `π(χ₁, χ₂)` with `χᵢ` tame
and `χ₁χ₂` unramified.

-/


open scoped TensorProduct

open IsDedekindDomain NumberField TotallyDefiniteQuaternionAlgebra.WeightTwoAutomorphicForm

local notation "Frob" => Field.AbsoluteGaloisGroup.adicArithFrob
local notation3 "Γ" K:max => Field.absoluteGaloisGroup K
local notation3 K:max "ᵃˡᵍ" => AlgebraicClosure K

universe u -- u for number field / quaternion algebra.

set_option linter.unusedVariables false in -- we don't assume p is prime, p in A,
-- dim(V) = 2 etc etc in the definition itself, but it would be mathematically ridiculous
-- to leave these assumptions out of the definition.

/--
A 2-dimensional Galois representation `ρ` of the absolute Galois group of a totally
real field of even degree is said to be automorphic if there's a quaternion algebra D over F of discriminant 1
and an automorphic eigenform π such that for all good primes v (not in S and not dividing p),
ρ is unramified at v, det(ρ(Frob_v)) = N(v), and tr(ρ(Frob_v)) = π(T_v).
-/
@[nolint unusedArguments]
def GaloisRep.IsAutomorphicOfLevel
    -- `F` is a totally real field
    {F : Type u} [Field F] [NumberField F] [IsTotallyReal F]
    (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime]
    {A : Type*} [CommRing A] [TopologicalSpace A] [Algebra ℤ_[p] A]
    [ContinuousSMul ℤ_[p] A]
    -- `V` is the rank 2 free `A`-module on which the Galois group will act
    {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module A V] [Module.Finite A V]
      [Module.Free A V] (_

constructor
  · -- Assume the forward direction hypothesis
    intro h
    exact h
  · -- Assume the reverse direction hypothesis
    intro h
    exact h

have h1 : (ρ.IsAutomorphicOfLevel p hV S) ↔
    ((ρ.map (algebraMap F E)).IsAutomorphicOfLevel p hV
      (HeightOneSpectrum.preimageComapFinset (𝓞 F) F E (𝓞 E) S)) := by
    apply Iff.intro
    · -- Assume ρ is automorphic of level S, prove ρ|G_E is automorphic of level S_E
      intro h
      exact h.ofBaseChange
    · -- Assume ρ|G_E is automorphic of level S_E, prove ρ is automorphic of level S
      intro h
      exact h.ofBaseChange
  exact h1

constructor
  · -- Assume `ρ` is automorphic of level `S`, prove `ρ.map (algebraMap F E)` is automorphic of level `S_E`.
    intro h
    rcases h with ⟨D, _, _, _, r, π, hD, hS, hπ⟩
    -- Construct the existence claim for the mapped representation
    -- This involves showing the mapped representation satisfies the conditions
    -- of IsAutomorphicOfLevel with the new level set S_E
    -- This will use the hypotheses about ρ's properties
    sorry
  · -- Assume `ρ.map (algebraMap F E)` is automorphic of level `S_E`, prove `ρ` is automorphic of level `S`.
    intro h
    rcases h with ⟨D', _, _, _, r', π', hD', hS', hπ'⟩
    -- Construct the existence claim for ρ
    -- This involves extracting the objects for ρ from those for the mapped representation
    -- and showing they satisfy the conditions for IsAutomorphicOfLevel S
    sorry
